blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Ear and Hearing Disorders?
Hearing loss คือ one of the most common congenital abnormalities — 1.5–2 per 1000 newborns; แต่ NICU infants สูงถึง 7.5/1000 ที่ GA 24 สัปดาห์. สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) EHDI 1-3-6 Rule: screen by 1 month → diagnose by 3 months → intervention by 6 months; (2) NICU infants → AABR (ไม่ใช่ OAE) เพราะ auditory neuropathy ที่ OAE ตรวจไม่เจอ; (3) ~50% ของ permanent HL ไม่มี risk factor ที่รู้จัก — universal screening จึงจำเป็น; (4) cCMV คือ cause ที่สำคัญของ delayed-onset progressive SNHL — ติดตามถึง 3 ปี; (5) auditory cortex plasticity สูงสุดใน 3.5 ปีแรก — cochlear implant ช้าเกิน = เสียโอกาส; (6) GJB2 (Connexin 26) = most common genetic cause of nonsyndromic SNHL.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย anatomy of normal hearing pathway จาก outer ear → cochlea → 8th nerve → brainstem → temporal lobe
แยก 4 types of hearing loss: sensorineural, auditory neuropathy, permanent conductive, transient conductive
รู้ degrees of hearing loss: mild 20–34 dB, moderate 35–49 dB, moderately severe 50–64 dB, severe 65–79 dB, profound >80 dB
เปรียบเทียบ OAE vs AABR: sensitivity, what each detects, และ ทำไม AABR ถึง preferred ใน NICU
ท่องจำ JCIH 11 risk factors for permanent hearing loss และ surveillance intervals
รู้ EHDI 1-3-6 Rule และ diagnostic audiology battery หลัง fail screen
ระบุ multidisciplinary workup: audiologist, ENT/otolaryngology, genetics, ophthalmology, developmental specialist
รู้ GJB2/Connexin 26, >400 syndromes associated with HL, และ genetic testing approach
อธิบาย 5 communication options + assistive technology (hearing aids จาก 1 month, cochlear implant ≥12 months)
ระบุ auditory cortex sensitive period: 3.5 years = window; หลังนั้น plasticity ลดลงมาก
รู้ rates of HL ใน NICU cohorts ตาม GA และ neonatal morbidities (BPD, brain injury, severe ROP, ECMO)
1. Anatomy of the Normal Hearing Pathway
Hearing loss คือ interference ใน transmission of sound ที่จุดใดก็ได้ใน pathway จาก outer ear → temporal lobe. Auditory pathway เริ่ม develop เร็วมาก:
Inner ear (otic placode): develops ~day 22 gestation — earliest developing structure ใน pathway
Middle ear + ossicles: derived from 1st and 3rd pharyngeal pouches; develop ~day 33
Outer ear: from dorsal portion of 1st pharyngeal cleft — ดังนั้น congenital HL มักพบร่วมกับ structural abnormalities ของ head, eyes, ears, nose, throat
Clinical implication: preauricular skin tag or pit → odds of hearing loss significantly increased (Roth Pediatrics 2008); atresia/microtia ของ pinna → strongly associated with permanent HL

rows: Pinna/auricle, ear canal, outer TM layer
Sound waves conducted through ear canal → TM → vibrations enter middle ear
Amplify + transmit vibrations to cochlear fluid; convert acoustic energy in air → compression waves in fluid
Convert fluid waves → electrochemical impulses → auditory nerve. Outer hair cells (OAE source). Inner hair cells (signal to nerve)
Balance and spatial orientation via vestibular nerve
Carry auditory sensory info from cochlea → brainstem
Continued transmission of electrical energy toward cortex
Process and interpret sound as language

blocks.text: 2. Types and Degrees of Hearing Loss
2.1 Types of Hearing Loss

rows: Pathology involving 8th nerve, outer hair cells, or inner hair cells of cochlea → impairs neuroconduction to brainstem
Pathology of myelinated fibers of 8th CN or inner hair cells. Outer hair cells INTACT (OAE present!). Impairs neuroconduction. Common in NICU infants
Anatomic obstruction of outer ear (atresia) or middle ear (ossicular fusion) → blocks sound transmission
Debris in ear canal or fluid in middle ear (otitis media with effusion) — reversible. Common in NICU (amniotic fluid, tube feeding prone position)
Combination of sensorineural/neural + conductive (transient or permanent)

blocks.text:
Auditory Neuropathy — NICU Trap
Auditory neuropathy มีอยู่บ่อยใน NICU infants. OAE จะ PASS (outer hair cells intact) แต่ AABR จะ FAIL เพราะ AN = problem ที่ inner hair cells หรือ auditory nerve. ดังนั้น ถ้า screen NICU ด้วย OAE เท่านั้น จะพลาด auditory neuropathy ทั้งหมด — นี่คือเหตุผลหลักที่ NICU ต้องใช้ AABR.
2.2 Degrees of Hearing Loss

rows: Difficulty in noisy environments; may miss soft speech
Difficulty understanding conversational speech without hearing aids
Speech understanding significantly impaired without amplification
Only loud speech/sounds perceived; hearing aids needed
Cochlear implant candidacy range; near-total hearing loss

blocks.text: 3. Newborn Hearing Screening — OAE vs AABR
3.1 สองวิธีหลักและความแตกต่าง
ทั้งสองวิธี = objective, non-invasive physiologic measures ที่ใช้ได้ใน newborns. แต่มีความแตกต่างสำคัญ:

rows: Physiologic response of OUTER hair cells of cochlea
Cochlear + auditory neural function up to brainstem
NO — outer hair cells intact in AN
40–45 dB HL (higher → may miss mild HL)

blocks.text: AABR threshold สูงกว่า (40–45 dB) → AABR may miss borderline or mild hearing loss compared to OAE (30–35 dB fail threshold)
Both methods false positive ใน transient conductive HL (middle ear fluid/amniotic fluid retained) → ↑ false positives ใน NICU infants
UK longitudinal cohort >17,000 neonates: 24% ที่ fail TEOAE แต่ pass AABR → ตรวจพบ HL >45 dB HL ด้วย diagnostic ABR (Levit Pediatrics 2015)
3.2 EHDI 1-3-6 Rule — หัวใจของ Newborn Hearing Screening

EHDI 1-3-6 Rule (AAP / JCIH 2019) — ท่องจำ
Screen by 1 month → Diagnose by 3 months → Early Intervention by 6 months of age. เป้าหมาย: ลด delayed language development, literacy, academic failure. NICU graduates ที่ fail hearing screen → diagnostic ABR prior to discharge (JCIH 2019). CDC 2018: >98% ของ US newborns ถูก screen. 70.1% ของ diagnosed HL ได้รับ EI by 6 months.
ก่อนมี EHDI: children diagnosed HL ≈ 19–31 months; หลัง EHDI: ≈ 2–4 months — ลดลงมาก (Yoshinaga-Itano 2001; Vohr 2002)
Hearing loss ที่พลาดจากทั้งสองวิธี: mild HL, isolated frequency range HL, progressive HL, late-onset HL — ดังนั้นต้อง ongoing surveillance ทุกราย
4. Risk Factors for Permanent Hearing Loss (JCIH 11 Factors)
~50% ของ infants ที่มี permanent HL ไม่มี known risk factor — จึงต้อง universal screening. แต่ infants ที่มี risk factor ต้องได้รับ enhanced surveillance.

rows: Caregiver concern re: hearing, speech, language, developmental delay
Family history of permanent childhood hearing loss
NICU care >5 days OR ECMO (regardless of duration)
Diagnostic ABR before NICU discharge; ECMO → eval by 3 months, then q12 months to school age
In utero infections: CMV, herpes, rubella, syphilis, toxoplasmosis
cCMV → eval by 3 months, yearly through age 3; others → by 9 months; Zika → AABR by 1 month
Craniofacial anomalies: pinna, ear canal, ear tags/pits, temporal bone anomalies
Physical findings associated with syndrome (e.g., white forelock, cleft lip/palate, micro-ophthalmia)
Syndromes with HL: Waardenburg, Alport, Pendred, Jervell-Lange-Nielsen, Usher, neurofibromatosis, osteopetrosis
Neurodegenerative disorders (Hunter syndrome) or sensory motor neuropathies (Friedreich ataxia, Charcot-Marie-Tooth)
Culture-positive postnatal infections: bacterial/viral meningitis (HSV, varicella)
No later than 3 months from event
Head trauma: basal skull/temporal bone fracture requiring hospitalization
No later than 3 months from event
No later than 3 months from event

blocks.text:
Aminoglycosides — Risk Factor Nuance
Aminoglycosides ≥5 days = risk factor → ใน risk factor 3 (NICU >5 days). Aminoglycosides <5 days = NOT a risk factor ตาม JCIH 2019. เช่นกัน perinatal asphyxia and exchange transfusion for hyperbilirubinemia ก็รวมอยู่ใน risk factor 3 regardless of length of NICU stay.
5. After Failed Newborn Screen — Diagnostic Audiology Protocol
5.1 Battery of Tests หลัง Fail Screen

1. Click-evoked ABR (condensation + rarefaction single-polarity): gold standard ใน NICU graduates + ถ้า risk indicators for AN; quantifies frequency-specific thresholds for air- and bone-conduction; ear-specific type + degree
2. OAE (distortion product or transient evoked): assess integrity ของ outer hair cells of cochlea — จำเป็นในการ identify AN (OAE present + ABR absent = AN)
3. Tympanometry (1000-Hz probe tone): assess middle ear function; 1000 Hz tone (ไม่ใช่ 226 Hz มาตรฐาน) เพราะ infant ear canal compliant ต่างกัน
4. Acoustic reflexes: important component of middle ear function; acoustic reflex absent in auditory neuropathy — ช่วย diagnosis
5. Visual Reinforcement Audiometry (VRA): gold standard for hearing threshold confirmation; cannot be done until developmental level 7–9 months; ไม่ได้ใช้ for fitting amplification alone
6. Behavioral observation: cross-check เท่านั้น; NOT adequate alone for determining HL or fitting amplification
5.2 Medical Work-Up หลัง Diagnosis
Visit with family ทันทีหลัง audiologist diagnose: review pregnancy + neonatal + family history; comprehensive physical exam focusing on craniofacial + syndromic features
Middle ear status สำคัญมาก: MEE (middle ear effusion) delayed diagnosis ของ permanent HL — ถ้า persistent: myringotomy tube placement ก่อนที่จะ confirm permanent HL
NICU infants: ↑ incidence of middle ear effusion → ↑ false positive screens; neonatal MEE = risk factor for chronic OME in year 1 (Doyle Otol Neurotol 2004)
6. Multidisciplinary Care — 3 Essential Specialties
6.1 Otolaryngology (ENT)
Comprehensive assessment of etiology + risk for progression + factors affecting management + guidance on amplification, assistive devices, and surgical intervention
Temporal bone imaging: HRCT (better for bony structures) vs MRI (better for cranial nerves + soft tissue); sedation + radiation (HRCT) = considerations
Cochlear implant candidacy evaluation: candidacy, timing, and surgical planning
6.2 Genetics
All families of children with confirmed HL → genetics evaluation + counseling (etiology, prognosis, associated disorders, recurrence risk)
GJB2 (Connexin 26) gene = most common genetic cause of nonsyndromic SNHL; autosomal recessive; highly prevalent in families with early-onset HL
Multigene panels for HL commercially available; >400 syndromes and genetic disorders associated with HL (Nance Ann NY Acad Sci 1991; Carey Indian J Pediatr 2016)
Three-generation family history review; ACMG guideline (Alford Genet Med 2014) for etiologic diagnosis of HL
6.3 Ophthalmology
At least one exam by ophthalmologist experienced in infants — vision impairment associated with HL; ophthalmologist may be first to identify syndromic features

⚠️ Jervell and Lange-Nielsen Syndrome — ECG Required
Children with profound sensorineural HL without identified etiology → obtain ECG to look for prolonged QT interval. Jervell and Lange-Nielsen syndrome: profound bilateral SNHL + prolonged QT → risk of sudden death (ventricular arrhythmia). ห้ามลืม: ECG เป็น required part of workup ใน profound SNHL without etiology.
30–40% ของ children with confirmed HL มี developmental comorbidities → developmental specialist referral as appropriate
7. Genetics of Hearing Loss — Syndromes ที่ต้องรู้
7.1 Nonsyndromic Genetic HL
GJB2 (Connexin 26): encodes gap junction protein; most common cause of nonsyndromic autosomal recessive SNHL; multiple mutations (35delG most common in Caucasians)
GJB6 (Connexin 30): second most common gap junction gene; digenic inheritance with GJB2 mutations possible
Autosomal dominant nonsyndromic HL (DFNA), recessive (DFNB), X-linked, mitochondrial — numerous loci; multigene panel testing recommended
7.2 Syndromic HL — ที่ Neonatologist ต้องรู้

rows: White forelock, heterochromia iridis, synophrys, dystopia canthorum (type I)
Enlarged vestibular aqueduct, thyroid goiter (may be absent in neonates)
Prolonged QTc → syncope/sudden death; ECG required
Congenital SNHL (type I) or progressive (II, III)
Coloboma, Heart defects, choanal Atresia, Growth retardation, Genital abnormalities, Ear abnormalities
Mandibulofacial dysostosis: malar hypoplasia, micrognathia, abnormal pinnae, choanal atresia

blocks.text: 7.3 Congenital CMV (cCMV) — Most Important Acquired Cause
cCMV = most common nongenetic cause of SNHL; unilateral or bilateral; early, progressive, and delayed-onset SNHL; can be symptomatic (12.6% HL) or asymptomatic (9% HL) — Goderis Pediatrics 2014
Surveillance: audiological evaluation by 3 months of age + yearly through age 3 (due to high rate of progressive loss)
Diagnosis of cCMV: PCR of saliva or urine within 3 weeks of birth = gold standard (Ross J Infect Dis 2014)
Treatment: valganciclovir for 6 months → ↓ progressive HL at 2 years (Kimberlin NEJM 2015 — not in this chapter but landmark reference)
8. Communication Options and Assistive Technology
8.1 5 Communication Options

rows: Residual hearing + amplification + visual support (speech reading/lip reading)
Based on listening skills alone; no visual cues (excludes lip reading)
Visual communication + 8 hand shapes in 4 placements near face to disambiguate lip reading
Combines all means: speech + sign simultaneously
Visual + manual language system (NOT a signed version of English)

blocks.text: Expert opinion: consider both sign and spoken language for all young children who are deaf to ensure language access. Bilingual children with HL who received dual-language support outperformed English-only support peers (Bunta LSHSS 2013; Bunta IJLCD 2016)
8.2 Hearing Aids
Can be fitted as young as 1 month of age; BTE (behind-the-ear) preferred in infants; computer-programmed gain adjustments per individual's frequency-specific thresholds
Follow-up every 6 weeks in year 1 → replace outgrown molds; surveillance for progression or fluctuation of thresholds
Tomblin et al. Ear Hear 2015: earlier age of fitting + higher daily duration + greater audibility → significantly higher early language skills in mild–severe HL cohort
Walker et al. JSLHR 2015: full-time hearing aid use (>8.7 h/day) → higher Peabody receptive vocabulary + CELF grammar in slight/mild HL 5–7 year olds vs part-time or non-users
FM systems: microphone (teacher/parent) + receiver worn by child; enhances hearing in noisy environments; signal = listening to speech inches away; ≤50 feet range
8.3 Cochlear Implants
FDA approved: children ≥12 months with severe-profound SNHL; 2019 expanded to single-sided deafness (SSD) + asymmetric HL; ~65,000 children implanted in US
Standard: adequate hearing aid trial 3–6 months before CI candidacy; lack of benefit with HA = eligibility criterion
Earlier implantation → higher likelihood of better speech + language development; early postimplant speech perception predicts long-term language + neurocognitive outcomes (Hunter JSLHR 2017)

⚠️ Cochlear Implant + Bacterial Meningitis Risk
Cochlear implant recipients at increased risk of bacterial meningitis; Streptococcus pneumoniae = most common pathogen. PCV13 at usual childhood schedule + Pneumovax-23 after age 2 (ACIP recommendation). Reefhuis J et al. NEJM 2003;349:435: established meningitis risk in CI recipients — led to vaccination guideline change.
9. Early Intervention and Brain Plasticity — สำคัญที่สุด
9.1 Auditory Cortex Sensitive Period

Auditory Cortex Plasticity Window — กดจำตัวเลขนี้
First 3.5 years of life = maximally sensitive + plastic สำหรับ development of auditory pathway + speech in response to cochlear implant. 3.5–7 years: plasticity drops to 7–30%. >7 years: plasticity <5%. ข้อมูลนี้มาจาก Sharma A et al. Hear Res 2005: central auditory development in children with CI. ดังนั้น: ยิ่ง diagnose เร็ว + intervene เร็ว = ยิ่ง maximize window of plasticity.

Cross-modal reorganization: DHH children without auditory stimulation → auditory cortex reorganized in response to visual + somatosensory input (Sharma 2005) — ยิ่ง HL นานก่อน intervention → auditory cortex 'captured' by other senses more

Sign babbling: deaf infants exposed to visual language (ASL) develop sign babbling at same rate hearing infants develop verbal babbling (Petitto Cognition 2004) — แสดงว่า brain ready to acquire language early ไม่ว่าจะ modality ไหน
9.2 Early Intervention Best Practices (JCIH 2019)
All infants with permanent HL → referred + enrolled in early intervention by 6 months; state EHDI programs = single point of entry

JCIH 12 best practice guidelines include: timely EI referral to providers skilled in early childhood deafness; partnerships with parents + Deaf/HH professionals; longitudinal developmental assessments; data systems for outcomes monitoring
Moeller Pediatrics 2000: enrollment in EI + greater family involvement → higher Peabody vocabulary scores at 5 years among DHH children in Nebraska (adjusted analyses)
Yoshinaga-Itano JDSE 2014: early quality intervention providing access to language (speech or ASL) in year 1 → improved language + developmental outcomes
Hart & Risley 1995: amount + quality of language input in home in first 3 years → determines language + cognitive outcomes; especially important for DHH children
10. NICU Infants — Risk Factors and Rates of Hearing Loss
10.1 Hearing Loss Rates by Gestational Age (Van Dommelen J Pediatr 2015)
Dutch NICU cohort 18,564 preterm infants 1998–2012. SGA infants <27 weeks also at increased risk.
10.2 Effect of Neonatal Morbidities (Schmidt JAMA 2003; J Pediatr 2015)
VLBW 500–1250 g (n=1514): overall deafness rate 2%; rates by morbidity:
BPD: 3.5% vs 1.4% (no BPD)
Major brain injury: 2.9% vs 2.2%
Severe ROP: 3.8% vs 2.2%
Multiple morbidities: deafness rate by number of risk factors: 0 RF: 1.3%; 1 RF: 3.6%; 2 RF: 5.4%; 3 RF: 25%
Robertson et al. Pediatrics 2009 (30-year study, ≤1250g/≤28 wk, n=1279):
Permanent HL: 3.1%; severe-profound: 1.9%
Progressive HL: 28%; 73% had other disabilities
Risk factors: prolonged O2 use, GI surgery, PDA ligation, low SES
Other significant NICU risk factors (controlling for confounders): dysmorphic features, low Apgar, sepsis, cerebral bleeding/infarction, severe asphyxia, ventilation ≥5 days, prolonged O2 use, ECMO
11. NICU Practical — Hearing Loss in NICU
Screen ด้วย AABR เท่านั้นใน NICU: ห้ามใช้ OAE เป็น sole screen ใน NICU; OAE จะพลาด auditory neuropathy ซึ่งพบบ่อยใน NICU infants (hyperbilirubinemia, asphyxia, prematurity)
Fail AABR ก่อน discharge → diagnostic ABR prior to NICU discharge: ไม่รอ outpatient referral — JCIH 2019 recommendation สำหรับ NICU graduates
Middle ear fluid ใน NICU: ↑ false positive screen;
Check 1000-Hz tympanometry; ตาม MEE closely ใน outpatient
Persistent MEE → myringotomy tubes ก่อน confirm permanent HL
cCMV suspect: PCR saliva/urine within 3 weeks of birth; ถ้า positive → audiology eval by 3 months + yearly surveillance to age 3; consider valganciclovir consult (ID/neonatology)
ECMO survivors: diagnostic audiology evaluation ≤3 months after ECMO + every 12 months to school age — high-risk group
Aminoglycoside use: ≥5 days = risk factor; <5 days = not a risk factor per JCIH 2019; document drug + duration in discharge summary สำหรับ primary care provider
Syndromic features: preauricular tag/pit → ↑ risk HL; pinna atresia/microtia → very high risk; ทุกรายที่ถูก diagnose HL → genetics consult + ECG (for Jervell-Lange-Nielsen QTc) + ophthalmology

Early intervention referral: family-centered; HL diagnosed → EI enrollment by 6 months; support family in communication choice (total communication → ASL + speech); bilingual families → dual-language EI support
12. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — OAE Present + ABR Absent = Auditory Neuropathy (ไม่ใช่ Normal)
Auditory neuropathy (AN): outer hair cells intact → OAE pass; inner hair cells or auditory nerve abnormal → ABR absent. ถ้า NICU infant pass OAE but fail ABR (หรือ abnormal morphology) → ไม่ใช่ technical error → diagnose AN. Management: hearing aids + ongoing monitoring; CI candidacy ขึ้นอยู่กับ progress. OAE alone จะพลาด AN ทั้งหมด — นี่คือเหตุผลหลัก NICU ต้องใช้ AABR.

PEARL 2 — 1-3-6 Rule: Screen 1M → Diagnose 3M → Intervene 6M
ท่องจำ: 1 month screen, 3 months diagnose, 6 months early intervention (EHDI/JCIH). ก่อน EHDI ใน 1980s: age of HL diagnosis ≈ 19–31 months → academic reading level = Grade 3 at high school graduation. หลัง EHDI: age ≈ 2–4 months. NICU graduates: diagnostic ABR ก่อน discharge ไม่รอ outpatient. 70% ของ diagnosed HL ได้รับ EI by 6M (CDC 2018).

PEARL 3 — 50% ของ HL ไม่มี Risk Factor → Universal Screen เท่านั้น
~50% ของ permanent HL ไม่มี known risk factor. ดังนั้น risk-factor screening เพียงอย่างเดียว = พลาดครึ่ง. Universal newborn hearing screening คือ solution เดียว. อีก 50% ที่ผ่าน screen แต่ develop late-onset/progressive HL จาก cCMV, genetic cause, noise, head trauma → ต้อง ongoing surveillance ทุกราย + ตอบสนองต่อ parental concern ทันที.

PEARL 4 — cCMV คือ Silent Cause ของ Progressive HL ถึง Age 3
cCMV: symptomatic 12.6% HL; asymptomatic cCMV ยังมี 9% HL และ progressive. HL อาจยังไม่มีตอน newborn screen ผ่าน → delayed onset. ดังนั้น: ทุก cCMV case → audiology eval by 3 months + annual follow-up to age 3. Diagnose: PCR saliva or urine ≤3 weeks of birth. หลัง 3 สัปดาห์ = ไม่สามารถ distinguish congenital vs postnatal (Goderis Pediatrics 2014).

PEARL 5 — Profound HL Without Etiology → ECG (QTc) ก่อน Cochlear Implant
Jervell and Lange-Nielsen syndrome: profound bilateral SNHL + prolonged QTc (KCNQ1, KCNE1 mutations — AR) → syncope + sudden cardiac death from ventricular tachyarrhythmia. ECG ต้องทำใน every child with profound SNHL without identified etiology. อีก reason: cochlear implant surgery ใน undiagnosed prolonged QTc = anesthesia risk. Schwartz Circulation 2006: natural history and molecular basis of JLNS.
13. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

NIH Consensus Statement 1993 Mar;11(1):1-24: Early identification of hearing impairment in infants and young children — foundational recommendation for universal newborn hearing screening; launched the EHDI era.

White KR, Vohr BR, Behrens TR. Semin Hearing 1993;14:18: Rhode Island demonstration project — first demonstrated feasibility of universal newborn hearing screening using OAE; led to national EHDI programs.
Morton CC, Nance WE. N Engl J Med 2006;354:2151: Newborn hearing screening — a silent revolution. Comprehensive review of genetics of HL + EHDI impact; GJB2 epidemiology and global significance.

JCIH Year 2019 position statement. J Early Hear Detect Interv 2019;4:1: Current gold-standard guidelines for EHDI programs — 1-3-6 goals, risk factor update, NICU-specific recommendations (diagnostic ABR before discharge), surveillance intervals per risk factor.

Yoshinaga-Itano C, Coulter D, Thomson V. Semin Neonatol 2001;6:521: Developmental outcomes with vs. without universal newborn hearing screening — early identification associated with significantly better language outcomes.

Sharma A, Dorman MF, Kral A. Hear Res 2005;203:134: Sensitive period for central auditory development — auditory cortex maximally plastic for CI up to 3.5 years; drops 3.5–7 years; <5% after 7 years. Defines urgency of early CI.

Tomblin JB et al. Ear Hear 2015;36(Suppl 1):76S: Language outcomes in children with mild-severe HL — earlier HA fitting + greater daily use + audibility all independently predict higher language; moderate-severe HL group lowest language scores 2–5 years.

Tomblin JB et al. Ear Hear 2020;41(4):775: Aided hearing moderates academic outcomes of children with mild-severe HL — hearing aid audibility is a modifiable variable for school-age academic outcomes.

Walker EA et al. JSLHR 2015;58:1611: Influence of hearing aid use in children with slight-mild HL — full-time HA use (>8.7 h/day) associated with significantly higher vocabulary + grammar vs part-time or no use in 5–7 year olds.

Moeller MP. Pediatrics 2000;106:E43: Early intervention and language development in deaf and hard of hearing — enrollment in EI + greater family involvement predicted higher vocabulary at 5 years in DHH children.

van Dommelen P et al. J Pediatr 2015;166:840: HL by gestational age in very preterm NICU neonates — 18,564 Dutch infants: rates range 7.5% at 24 wk to 1.2% at 31 wk; risk consistently increases with decreasing gestation.

Robertson CMT et al. Pediatrics 2009;123:e797: 30-year study of NICU extreme prematurity: permanent HL 3.1% (severe-profound 1.9%); 28% progressive; 73% had other disabilities; risk factors include prolonged O2, GI surgery, PDA ligation, low SES.
Schmidt B et al. JAMA 2003;289:1124: BPD, brain injury, severe ROP and HL at 18 months in VLBW <1250g; 0 RF: 1.3%, 1 RF: 3.6%, 2 RF: 5.4%, 3 RF: 25% — dose-response with morbidity count.
Goderis J et al. Pediatrics 2014;134:972: cCMV and HL — systematic review; symptomatic cCMV: 18% HL; asymptomatic: 9% HL; delayed-onset HL 0–50%; mandates ongoing audiological surveillance.

Reefhuis J et al. N Engl J Med 2003;349:435: Risk of bacterial meningitis in children with cochlear implants — established increased S. pneumoniae meningitis risk; led to ACIP vaccination guideline update (PCV13 + Pneumovax-23).

Alford RL et al. Genet Med 2014;16:347: ACMG guideline for clinical evaluation + etiologic diagnosis of hearing loss — systematic approach including GJB2 testing, multigene panels, imaging, and syndromic HL workup.

Ross SA et al. J Infect Dis 2014;210:1415: Detection of cCMV by real-time PCR of saliva or urine specimens — saliva is more sensitive and practical for newborn cCMV screening; must be done within 3 weeks of birth.
14. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: ทำไม NICU infants ต้องใช้ AABR ไม่ใช่ OAE เป็น screening method? มีข้อยกเว้นไหม?

ตอบ: AABR ตรวจ auditory neuropathy (AN) ได้ — OAE ไม่สามารถตรวจได้. AN: outer hair cells intact → OAE PASS, แต่ inner hair cells หรือ auditory nerve abnormal → AABR FAIL. AN พบบ่อยมากขึ้นใน NICU infants (hyperbilirubinemia, asphyxia, prematurity). OAE screen: จะ false negative ใน AN ทั้งหมด. ไม่มีข้อยกเว้น — NICU = AABR เท่านั้น. นอกจากนี้ AABR ยัง detect sensorineural HL + conductive HL ด้วย (เหมือน OAE) แต่ AABR threshold for fail สูงกว่า (40–45 dB vs 30–35 dB) → may miss mild HL.
Q: EHDI 1-3-6 Rule คืออะไร? NICU graduates มี additional requirement อะไรเพิ่มเติม?

ตอบ: Screen by 1 month → Diagnose by 3 months → Intervention by 6 months (JCIH 2019; AAP EHDI). NICU additional requirement: NICU infants who do not pass hearing screen → diagnostic ABR prior to NICU discharge — ไม่ต้องรอ outpatient visit. ECMO: diagnostic audiology ≤3 months after ECMO + every 12 months to school age. cCMV: audiology by 3 months + yearly to age 3. Caregiver concern: referral ทันที regardless of prior screen result.
Q: Infant มี OAE present แต่ ABR absent — diagnosis + management?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: auditory neuropathy (AN) / auditory dyssynchrony (AD). OAE present = outer hair cells intact; ABR absent = inner hair cells or auditory nerve dysfunction → neural transmission impaired. Management: hearing aids trial + close monitoring; acoustic reflex จะ absent ใน AN; VRA เมื่อ developmental age ≥7–9 months; cochlear implant หาก HA benefit insufficient; etiology workup (hyperbilirubinemia + exchange transfusion, perinatal asphyxia, congenital CMV, genetic); genetics consult; prognosis variable — some AN infants improve over time.
Q: Infant อายุ 2 สัปดาห์ ถูก diagnose cCMV (asymptomatic) — hearing monitoring plan?

ตอบ: cCMV asymptomatic: HL rate ~9% + delayed-onset/progressive HL สูง (Goderis 2014). Plan: audiological evaluation by 3 months of age; yearly audiological evaluation through age 3 years (due to progressive loss); ต้องไม่ relax เพราะ newborn screen ผ่าน — screen อาจผ่านก่อน HL onset. Consider valganciclovir (consult ID/neonatology) — Kimberlin NEJM 2015: 6M valganciclovir → ↓ progressive HL + improved neurodevelopmental outcomes ใน symptomatic cCMV.
Q: Profound bilateral SNHL diagnosed in infant — workup steps in order?

ตอบ: Step 1: audiology diagnostic battery (ABR air/bone + OAE + tympanometry 1000 Hz + acoustic reflexes). Step 2: genetics consult + GJB2/Connexin 26 testing + multigene HL panel. Step 3: ECG — rule out Jervell and Lange-Nielsen (prolonged QTc + profound bilateral SNHL = sudden death risk). Step 4: ophthalmology evaluation. Step 5: ENT/otolaryngology — temporal bone imaging (HRCT vs MRI); CI candidacy planning. Step 6: cCMV PCR saliva/urine ถ้า age ≤3 weeks. Step 7: early intervention referral — enrolled by 6 months.
Q: Infant อายุ 3 วัน — fail AABR bilateral, OAE pass bilateral. เหตุการณ์นี้หมายความว่าอะไร?

ตอบ: This pattern = auditory neuropathy (AN). OAE pass = outer hair cells intact (cochlea functional). AABR fail = neural dysfunction beyond cochlea (inner hair cells or 8th nerve). ไม่ใช่ false positive จาก middle ear fluid เพราะ OAE ก็ผ่าน (MEE จะ affect both OAE and AABR). This is true AN until proven otherwise. Actions: diagnostic ABR before discharge + acoustic reflexes + tympanometry; etiology workup; family counseling; hearing aids trial + monitoring; early intervention referral.
Q: Jervell and Lange-Nielsen syndrome คืออะไร? เกี่ยวข้องกับ hearing loss อย่างไร?

ตอบ: Autosomal recessive; KCNQ1 or KCNE1 mutations — these potassium channel genes critical for endocochlear potential AND cardiac repolarization. Clinical: profound bilateral SNHL + prolonged QTc (>500 ms) → ventricular tachyarrhythmia (torsades de pointes) → syncope + sudden death. ดังนั้น ECG ใน every child with profound bilateral SNHL without known etiology. Management: beta-blockers ± ICD; HA or CI for HL. Schwartz Circulation 2006: natural history — sudden death risk highest in first decade.
Q: Cochlear implant timing — ideal age? ทำไมช้าเกินไปจึงไม่ดี?

ตอบ: FDA approved ≥12 months. Ideal: as early as possible within candidacy criteria — earlier implantation → higher likelihood better speech + language (Vlastarakos IJPO 2010). Brain reason: auditory cortex maximally plastic for CI response in first 3.5 years (Sharma 2005). 3.5–7 years: plasticity 7–30%; >7 years: <5%. Cross-modal reorganization after prolonged sound deprivation reduces auditory cortex response. Prerequisites: ≥3–6 months adequate HA trial without benefit; severe-profound SNHL bilateral; ECG normal (Jervell-Lange-Nielsen screen); vaccination current (PCV13 + Pneumovax-23 post-age 2).
Q: เด็กอายุ 5 ปี ผ่าน newborn screen แต่ parents concern ว่าไม่ยอม respond ต่อ soft sounds — ควรทำอะไร?

ตอบ: Referral for hearing testing ทันที — parental/caregiver concern คือ JCIH Risk Factor #1 และ highly predictive of hearing loss. ผ่าน newborn screen ไม่ป้องกัน late-onset HL. HL prevalence ↑ จาก ~1.2/1000 ใน newborns → 3.65/1000 by school age. เหตุสำคัญของ late-onset HL: cCMV (progressive), genetic causes, ototoxicity, meningitis, head trauma. Audiology evaluation ทันที; หากยืนยัน HL → genetics + ENT + ophthalmology + EI. ห้าม reassure ว่า 'screen ผ่านแล้วปลอดภัย'.
Day 98 Preview

Curriculum Milestone — Completing Part XX: Eyes and Ears
Day 97 เป็น chapter สุดท้ายของ Part XX — Eyes and Ears ใน Avery 11th ed. ชุด PDF ที่มีครอบคลุม Ch 96 (Eye and Vision Disorders) + Ch 97 (Ear and Hearing Disorders) — sensory organ pair ที่ screened ทุก newborn ก่อน discharge. ขั้นตอนต่อไป: ทบทวน Days 96–97 ร่วมกัน (Eyes + Ears) เพื่อ integrate sensory screening protocol หรือ request chapter จาก Fanaroff/Nelson ที่ต้องการ. ก็ยังสามารถขอ pure protocol lessons (e.g., hydrocortisone in BPD, PICC line care, NEC pathway) ได้ตลอดเวลาโดยไม่ต้องอิง textbook chapter.
